Ad Text:
About Us

Adaptiv is a true Kiwi success story! Recognised for our commitment to premium client delivery, we provide world-class integration, AI consulting and development service, along with data and analytics. We have a wealth of development and integration experience in moving clients from around the world onto cloud platforms.




Our integration work focuses on the exciting challenge of designing innovative API-led and event driven solutions. Leveraging the latest technologies, such as Databricks, MuleSoft, Boomi, Azure, and Solace, we develop services that unlock access to existing data within legacy business systems in new and ingenious ways.




These solutions enable organizations to drive major process efficiencies, engineer new operational workflows, and extract deeper insights from their data. Ultimately, this empowers businesses to operate more effectively and engage with their customers in modern, cutting-edge ways. Our services also include cloud consultancy, round-the clock middleware support, integration development, application lifecycle management, load and performance testing and customised integration training.




Based in our stylish Southbank offices in Melbourne, our team enjoys a collaborative, people-first culture where curiosity, initiative, and growth are valued. We combine serious technical capability with a down-to-earth, can-do approach and we’re looking for someone who shares that mindset.





About the Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Senior Data Engineer / Consultant to join our growing Data Engineering practice here at Adaptiv Integration to drive the delivery of data engineering engagements on behalf of our clients. In this role, you will ensure high-quality technical outcomes, foster strong client collaboration, and provide effective support to delivery teams.




This position combines advanced data engineering expertise with consulting leadership. You will own engineering delivery across assigned engagements, contribute to solution design within architectural guidelines, and mentor junior and intermediate team members to maintain consistent quality and standards.




This role will be responsible for the delivery of cutting-edge Databricks technology, driving innovative data engineering solutions for clients. You will leverage your expertise to design, implement, and optimise Databricks-based pipelines and solutions, ensuring high-quality technical outcomes and efficient, scalable data architectures.




The role is highly hands-on and delivery-focused, requiring autonomous execution and a strong emphasis on billable contribution.




What You’ll Be Doing

Build, maintain, and optimise data pipelines across lakes, lakehouses, and warehouses.
Write, test, and maintain high-quality code; develop transformations for reporting, analytics, and AI/ML.
Apply data modelling principles; troubleshoot and resolve issues efficiently.
Work with Databricks, Delta Live Tables, Lakeflow, Git, and CI/CD pipelines.
Implement automated testing, data validation, and performance optimisation.
Lead unit and integration testing; ensure solutions meet quality and documentation standards.
Support knowledge sharing and improvement of internal frameworks and templates.
Act as primary engineering contact, leading technical discussions and translating business needs into technical tasks.
Communicate progress, risks, and blockers; contribute to estimation, scoping, and planning.
Mentor and coach junior/intermediate team members; conduct peer reviews.
Ensure accountability for code quality, documentation, and adherence to best practices.
Collaborate with architects on technical solutions and delivery planning.
Participate in learning sessions and knowledge sharing; adopt modern data concepts (AI/ML, data visualisation, cloud-native).
Contribute to process, template, and documentation improvements; pursue relevant training and certification.




What You’ll Bring

6+ years of professional experience in data engineering or a related field.

At least 4 years of hands-on experience developing data pipelines and transformations using Databricks, including Delta Live Tables, notebooks, and workflows.

Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric concepts and experience contributing to projects that incorporate Fabric components.

Proficiency in Python and SQL for data transformation and analysis.
Understanding of data modelling including Star and Snowflake schema design for analytics workloads.
Knowledge of Medallion architecture principles and well-architected, scalable data solution design.

Experience applying DevOps practices such as version control, CI/CD, and environment automation.

Experience with testing and data quality frameworks to ensure reliable, accurate data delivery.

Experience with visualisation and data consumption concepts, including Power BI or similar tools.


Relevant industry certifications (Databricks Professional and Databricks Associate) or a demonstrated commitment to ongoing professional development.

 

If you are looking for your next role with an organisation where you can elevate your career, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

Location: Pakenham, West Gippsland & Latrobe Valley VIC
Ad Text:
AI Systems & Automation Developer

Grove is looking for an AI Systems & Automation Developer to help build the internal tools, workflows and technical infrastructure that will support the next stage of our business.

This is a hands-on development role for a technically capable graduate, early-career developer or systems-minded builder who can take clear business objectives and turn them into practical, scalable internal tools.

We know the business problems we want to solve. We are looking for someone who can design the technical infrastructure and build the tools to solve them.

This role will work across estimating, design, procurement, production, resource planning and project delivery, developing internal systems that improve speed, accuracy, visibility and consistency across the business.

This is an opportunity to help build Grove’s internal AI and automation capability from the ground up.




What you’ll be doing

Understanding business objectives set by senior management and translating them into technical solutions.

Designing the internal architecture required to support AI, automation and internal tool development.

Selecting appropriate development environments, platforms, APIs, databases and workflow tools.

Building internal tools across estimating, design, procurement, production, resource planning and project delivery.

Developing AI-assisted workflows using tools such as OpenAI, Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Replit, Retool or similar platforms.

Creating practical tools that improve speed, accuracy, consistency and visibility.

Building prototypes quickly, testing them with users, and refining them into reliable internal systems.

Structuring data and workflows so tools can be maintained, reused and scaled.

Developing dashboards, internal applications, automations and reporting tools.

Documenting the architecture, logic, workflows and user instructions for each tool developed.

Skills and experience

The ideal candidate will have:

Coding, software development or automation experience.

Ability to use AI-assisted coding tools to build working tools or applications.

Strong understanding of how to structure workflows, data and systems.

Ability to translate business objectives into technical requirements.

Ability to work independently and make technical recommendations.

Strong communication skills with non-technical users.

Experience with any of the following would be highly regarded:

Python, JavaScript, SQL, APIs or database tools.

OpenAI, Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Replit, Retool, Make, Zapier or similar.

ERP systems, spreadsheets, project management tools or document processing.

AI agents, structured prompts, document automation and internal business systems.




What success looks like

Grove has a clear internal architecture for AI and automation.

Internal tools are built in a structured, scalable way.

Business objectives from senior management are converted into working systems, in a timely fashion.

Tools are adopted by estimating, design, procurement, production and planning teams.

Accuracy, visibility and consistency improve across key workflows.

Systems are documented and capable of being expanded over time.

About Grove

Grove is a privately owned Australian business with operations across modular construction, hire, transport, property and motorsport.

We are continuing to invest in better systems, stronger processes and more efficient ways of working across the group. This role will sit at the intersection of technology and operations, building tools that directly impact how the business estimates, designs, procures, manufactures, plans and delivers work.

For the right person, this is a rare opportunity to build an internal AI and automation function from the ground up inside a large, practical and fast-moving business.

AI Engineer
LK Property | Melbourne, VIC

Most AI ‘engineers’ write strategies. This role builds the thing.

LK Group is one of Australia’s most ambitious diversified groups — spanning the NBL, luxury property, premium retail, hospitality, and technology. We’ve just formed a dedicated AI team with a clear mandate: move fast, build well, and create measurable value across our portfolio. This is the role that makes that happen.
  
You’re not here to advise. You’re here to ship. If you thrive when you’re building real systems that go live and create outcomes — and you want to do that at scale, with autonomy, inside a group that genuinely backs its people — keep reading.
  
About Us

Few groups move as fast or think as broadly as LK Group. Our businesses range from the National Basketball League, to major retailers such as Daniel’s Donuts, luxury property development, HR and recruitment, exciting digital and technology startups and much more. We aspire to achieve success and professionalism throughout our established subsidiary companies, and within our accomplished and integrated team, to deliver an exceptional experience to clients and businesses. Today, the Group employs thousands of people across the world who are truly committed to those values.
  
The Role

As our founding AI Engineer, you’re a core member of a newly formed AI Value Creation team, reporting to the GM AI Value Creation. Your job is to design, build, and deploy AI agents and automation pipelines that save real time across LK Group’s Support Team and Brand Portfolio — and then keep building.

Day to day, you’ll be:

Designing and deploying AI agents natively within Anthropic’s Claude platform
Integrating AI services into existing business systems via APIs, webhooks, and data pipelines
Building and maintaining automated workflow pipelines using n8n, Zapier, Make, and Shopify Flow
Testing agents against real business scenarios and resolving edge cases before they go live
Monitoring agent performance — reliability, latency, error rates — and keeping the standard high
Building and maintaining a library of reusable prompt templates, workflows, and system instructions
Writing clear technical documentation — README files, runbooks, integration guides — that others can actually follow
Supporting responsible AI practices across every build — data privacy, accuracy, and quality control embedded from the start  
This is a founding role. The infrastructure you build will define how AI gets done at LK Group.

Who We’re Looking For

We want a builder — someone who comes alive when they’re shipping, thinks in pipelines and agents, and has the engineering rigour to make things reliable and built to last.
  
You’re the right fit if you:

Have proven, hands-on experience building and deploying AI systems — what you’ve shipped matters more than how long you’ve been doing it
Have production experience integrating large language models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Copilot) into real applications — not just consumer or chat use
Are experienced with prompt engineering and LLM configuration: system prompts, tool use, multi-step chains, and output evaluation
Have built production workflows with n8n, Make, Zapier, or equivalent — things that actually work in production, not just prototypes
Have strong working knowledge of REST APIs, webhooks, authentication patterns, and data pipelines
Exercise strong engineering judgement — you choose the right tool, scope builds pragmatically, and debug systematically
Hold a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field — or have a portfolio that speaks for itself
And just as important — you bring these to work every day:
  
Entrepreneurial energy 
You move fast, back yourself, and don’t wait for perfect conditions to start building.

Professional rigour
You hold yourself to a high standard. Documentation is clear, code is clean, and what you ship works.

Genuine curiosity
You care about what’s possible with AI — not just what’s been done before and you bring that energy to every build.

Clarity in communication
You make complex technical concepts accessible to non-technical stakeholders without losing precision.
  
What’s on Offer

A founding role with real scope — you’re shaping how AI gets built across an entire group
Genuine autonomy — a mandate to move fast, backed by a team that trusts you to execute
Exposure across a genuinely diverse portfolio of businesses — property, sport, retail, tech, and more
A newly formed AI team at the start of something significant
Full-time, Monday to Friday — based at LK Group Head Office
Competitive salary package, commensurate with experience
Ready to build something real?

If you read this and felt it — apply. Send us your CV and tell us what you’ve built.

Job Title: Software Services AI Solutions Engineer II
Ad Text:
All Orbits, All Planets - Join us on our journey

Gilmour Space is a leading Australian space company, developing launch vehicles, satellite platforms, advanced propulsion technologies; and launching from our licensed orbital spaceport in Bowen, North Queensland.

Are you a creative and experienced Software Services AI Solutions Engineer II looking for a dynamic role where your skills will make a real impact? Have you ever dreamt of building rockets right here in Australia? Well now’s your chance! Gilmour Space is seeking a talented individual to join our innovative team to send rockets to Space! Conveniently located between Brisbane and the Gold Coast, our new state-of-the-art facility, purpose-built for designing and manufacturing rockets in-house, is equipped with the latest technology and tools, providing an ideal environment for innovation and excellence. So, what are you waiting for? Join us and be a part of a forward-thinking team dedicated to pushing the boundaries of space exploration and let’s send Australia to Space together!

How you will contribute to our mission in getting Australia to space:

The below is designed to give you an idea of the role you will play. There will be many more exciting and innovative things to do:

Define and prioritize AI improvements aligned to business needs.
Hands-on development, testing and deployment of AI solutions and workflows through structured and achievable work plans to solve complex business challenges.
Stay across current and emerging AI technologies, particularly generative and agentic AI, and apply that knowledge to drive change.
Analyse and integrate with existing API endpoints to deliver AI-powered automation.
Develop and enhance AI agentic capabilities to augment and automate workflows.
Build automated pipelines and optimise model performance and system reliability.
Evaluate and recommend where AI can deliver measurable business value across existing processes.
Articulate complex AI concepts clearly to stakeholders without technical backgrounds.
Coordinate with all parts of the business including engineering, supply chain and finance to identify potential improvements through AI assistance and learning.
Produce and maintain clear technical documentation and user guides
Evaluate training materials to support the adoption and understanding of AI tools across the business.
Ensure that engineering activities are conducted and documented in accordance with Gilmour Space policies, plans and procedures.
Other duties as requested by Management.

Minimum Qualifications, Skills and Experience:

Bachelor's degree in artificial intelligence, machine learning, software, computer science, and/or relevant experience.
Solid software engineering fundamentals including code quality, databases, APIs/integrations and delivery hygiene.
Ability to operate in dynamic, fast paced environments.
Demonstrated experience or strong foundational knowledge in AI/ML concepts, including generative AI, large language models and agentic frameworks.
Desired Qualifications, Skills and Experience:
Understanding of Machine Learning concepts and growing knowledge in Artificial Intelligence and Large Language Models (LLMs).
Understanding of model evaluation, prompt design, and non-deterministic system behaviour.
Experience working with cloud-based AI platforms.
Ability to integrate AI solutions with data platforms, pipelines and governance frameworks.
Interest in the development and maintenance of policies, standards and guidelines for AI/ML development practices, data governance and responsible AI use across the organisation.
Strong critical thinking skills with the ability to objectively evaluate AI solutions, question outputs and distinguish genuine value from hype.
Understanding of AI risk, bias, explainability and safe deployment practices.
Experience working in Agile delivery environments.
Familiarity developing full-stack, production quality applications.
A proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and practical outcomes.
Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders. 
Familiarity with any of C/C++, Golang, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript.
Experience interacting with RESTful and GraphQL APIs.
Understanding of Systems Engineering practices.
Interest in developing testing and evaluation strategies for non-deterministic systems, including prompt regression testing and output quality benchmarks.
Experience using git workflows and version control practices.
Awareness of cybersecurity principles and the ability to develop and deploy AI solutions with secure coding practices, data protection and access controls in mind.

How to Apply:

Applicants must be an Australian Citizen/Permanent Resident or have an appropriate Australian work visa or work entitlements.

If you are a proactive team player with a passion to get Australia to space and a desire to succeed, please apply now by clicking the apply button and including your resume and cover letter or head to our careers page: www.gspace.com/career

Gilmour Space Technologies is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members. We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples for all positions.

NTT Data are seeking a highly experienced System Integration Architect to lead the design, integration and delivery of complex, large-scale networked system within a secure government environment.

2 year contract
Canberra location – must be able to work onsite
Due to the secure nature of the work, only candidates holding a current, active NV1 security clearance will be considered

This role focuses on systems-of-systems integration, network architecture, and platform interoperability, supporting advanced development, testing and innovation initiatives. You will work across distributed environments, ensuring systems operate seamlessly and securely while enabling continuous improvement and capability uplift.

Key Responsibilities

Design, build, implement and manage enterprise and distributed WAN/LAN environments (TCP/IP, IEEE 802.3 / 802.11)
Architect and support virtualised server environments (VMware, Hyper‑V, KVM)
Design and manage secure network architectures, leveraging tools such as ForeScout, Splunk and McAfee
Lead the design and integration of cross-domain and multi-environment solutions
Develop and maintain automation, scripting and CI/CD pipelines to support system innovation
Perform system integration design, analysis and architecture alignment across complex environments
Manage configuration, change and release management across development, test and production environments
Integrate new releases and third-party systems into existing platforms
Evaluate and integrate MOTS, COTS and emerging technologies into enterprise systems
Produce high-quality technical documentation, architectures and engineering artefacts (including BOMs)
Conduct network planning, modelling and performance analysis
Troubleshoot and resolve network, system and infrastructure issues, including security vulnerabilities
Design and implement security controls and countermeasures
Research and develop new tools, systems and technical solutions
Develop and deliver training materials and technical enablement sessions
Provide Level 3 support for complex systems and critical operational activities
Support innovation initiatives, including emerging technologies such as secure wireless and immersive platforms
Undertake additional tasks as required to support project delivery

Key Requirements

Tertiary qualifications in Network Engineering, Systems Engineering, or a related discipline
10+ years’ experience in network engineering, systems integration or architecture roles
 Strong experience across WAN/LAN technologies and enterprise networks
Proven expertise with Cisco networking technologies
Hands-on experience with virtualisation platforms (VMware, Hyper‑V, KVM)
Experience designing and implementing secure, multi-environment architectures
Strong understanding of systems integration within complex, distributed environments
Experience working in highly secure, regulated or mission-critical environments (e.g. Defence, government, or critical infrastructure)
Exposure to deployable, tactical or operational network environments highly regarded
Experience integrating systems across multi-domain or federated network environments
Familiarity with cross-domain solutions and secure information exchange patterns is desirable
Experience with automation, scripting and CI/CD pipelines
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including technical documentation and training delivery
Ability to work independently and within complex stakeholder environments

 





Join our growing global team and accelerate your career with us. Apply today.

For more information about this or other opportunities, please contact Stephanie Hodak. Please quote our job reference number 668887.

About us

NTT Ltd. is a leading global technology services company. Working with organizations around the world, we achieve business outcomes through intelligent technology solutions. For us, intelligent means data driven, connected, digital and secure. As a global ICT provider, we employ more than 40,000 people in a diverse and dynamic workplace that spans 57 countries, trading in 73 countries and delivering services in over 200 countries and regions

NTT is proud to be an equal opportunity employer with a global culture that embraces diversity. We are committed to providing an environment free of unfair discrimination and harassment. We do not discriminate based on age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, veteran status, or other protected category.

As a Data Integration Specialist, you will be the technical lead for deploying and maintaining high-performance data integration layers. You will drive the design and development of cloud-based services, ensuring our integration platform (Azure iPaaS) is secure, scalable, and robust.

Key Responsibilities

Create and support solutions using Azure Logic Apps, API Management (APIM), Event Grid, and Azure Functions.

Develop secure Microservices and optimize API performance using OData, REST, and SOAP.

Monitor platform performance and lead troubleshooting for API-related issues.

Guide design activities, conduct peer reviews, and organize workloads within the project team.

Improve data access protocols and maintain an inventory of the enterprise integration landscape.

About You

To be successful in this role, you will bring a proactive attitude and a deep technical toolkit. We are looking for:

A Bachelor's degree in IT, Computer Science, or Management Information Systems (or equivalent experience) and a current NSW Driver's License.

Proven experience with Azure iPaaS (Logic Apps, Service Bus, APIM) and automating build/release cycles.

Proficiency in SQL, KQL, Postman, and Python.

Strong background in API design principles (versioning, error handling) and security (OAuth, JWT).

Familiarity with modern platforms like Databricks or Snowflake is highly regarded.

At Randstad Digital, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

About the Role

This is an opportunity for an experienced Automation Engineer to step into a hands-on, client-facing role delivering process automation solutions across manufacturing environments.

You’ll be involved across the full project lifecycle - from development through to commissioning - with the autonomy to run your own workstreams and contribute to project success on-site.

Projects are varied in size and complexity, ranging from short continuous improvement upgrades through to larger commissioning phases requiring extended site presence.

Location: South east Melbourne

Key responsibilities
Design and develop PLC and SCADA systems within FMCG manufacturing environments.
Deliver end-to-end project work including testing, commissioning and optimisation.
Work across multiple technologies and automation platforms.
Attend client sites for commissioning (typically 1 week to 1 month depending on project size).
Collaborate with internal teams and client stakeholders during project delivery.
Support continuous improvement initiatives and smaller upgrade projects.
The ideal candidate for this position will have experience in the following:
3-5 years’ experience in industrial/process automation.
Strong PLC experience (Rockwell/Allen Bradley and Siemens preferred).
Exposure to SCADA platforms such as FactoryTalk, Ignition, AVEVA or Citect.
Proven track record in end-to-end project delivery. 
Experience within FMCG, food & beverage or similar manufacturing industries.
Understanding of batch control and MES systems (S88/S95 highly regarded).
Basic database/SQL knowledge.
Comfortable working on-site and travelling when required.

To apply online, please click on the apply function. Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Nicole Tianihad on ntianihad@sharpandcarter.com.au

We are seeking an experience HIL (Hardware-in-the-Loop) Engineer to support the development and validation of vehicle electronic systems within a global automotive program. This role is responsible for the design, integration, and commissioning of HIL test environments, enabling robust off-vehicle verification of control modules and features. You will interpret vehicle architectures, define simulator interfaces, and work closely with technicians to build representative test platforms. In collaboration with feature owners and cross-functional teams, you will develop simulation models, fault insertion strategies, and automated test capabilities to ensure systems meet performance, quality, and program timing requirements.

Key Responsibilities

Lead onsite facility build and commissioning activities.

Identify vehicle parts and facility hardware required for test facility and manage the delivery.

Create the electrical hardware design to interface vehicle modules with the test facility.

Create Model design, integration and build the executable using dSPACE toolset.

Support test development and execution throughout the program lifecycle


Required Skills & Knowledge

Previous Ford experience highly desirable.

Minimum 5 years' experience in a System Analyst (HIL) role. (Mandatory)

Tertiary qualification in Mechanical / Aerospace engineering, or similar.

Automotive or similar background with understanding of electrical interfaces, schematics and vehicle networking protocols. (Mandatory)

Capable of fault finding to root cause facility design and model or vehicle hardware issues during commissioning and test execution. (Mandatory)

Skilled with Matlab, Simulink, dSPACE development toolchains such as Control Desk, Automation Desk, Vector. (Mandatory)

Flexibility to communicate across the Product Development domains, from Design Engineer to Feature Owner and test requestor.

Equally comfortable working with peers overseas to adopt prior knowledge as working independently to generate solutions.

Apply today to accelerate your career in automotive engineering.

Please note: Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Job Title: L2 & L3 MSP Engineers - Modern Workplace/Azure/Security/Networking
Ad Text:

Modern Workplace/Security/Networking!
$80k - $120k + Super + Training
